title: Title
description: Project update cover slide — May 2026, Traci Nathans-Kelly and Dr Simon Wang, terracotta gradient.
speakerNotes: Welcome Traci and collaborators. This is a snapshot of where we are — everything covered here either already exists or is one step from being done.

title: Where We Are
description: Four completed deliverables: Listening Demo, Content Library, Authors Found, Permission Letter Template.
speakerNotes: These four things are already done — not planned, done. The listening demo is live, all 127 articles are catalogued, and the permission letter is ready to send the moment you're happy with it.

title: Live Demo
description: The learner experience — AI-narrated audio, comprehension check, xAPI tracking, PDF certificate.
speakerNotes: Let me show you the demo. A learner clicks play, listens to the AI-narrated overview, answers a few comprehension questions, and receives a certificate. Every step is recorded in xAPI.

title: Content Library
description: 127 ProComm resources classified — 65 Articles, 44 Resource Links, 9 Podcasts. Top authors by article count.
speakerNotes: We went through every single article on the ProComm resources page — all 127. 65 are full articles suitable for conversion into lessons. And the top six authors account for about half the content.

title: Author Outreach
description: Permission letter plan — Traci as sender, 14 confirmed emails, attribution terms, trust signal.
speakerNotes: The key insight here is that the letters come from you as IEEE ProComm VP Content — not from a researcher at HKBU. That trust signal is huge. We've already confirmed 14 email addresses.

title: Research Study
description: Three research questions — comprehension, engagement, xAPI learning analytics. Traci's suggestion.
speakerNotes: This study design comes directly from Traci's suggestion. Three clean research questions that fit naturally into what the platform already produces — no extra instrumentation needed beyond what xAPI gives us.

title: Publication Pipeline
description: Three outputs — ProComm 2026 workshop paper (done), TPC Teaching Case (June), companion empirical study (proposed).
speakerNotes: One platform, three publications. The workshop paper is already submitted. The Teaching Case is in preparation for June. The empirical study is the one we'd co-author together.

title: Timeline
description: April–Fall 2026 milestones — paper done, outreach in May, Teaching Case in June, ProComm Edmonton July 12–15.
speakerNotes: The camera-ready deadline was April 30 — that's already done. The most pressing next step is the outreach in May, which is why we're meeting now.

title: Next Steps
description: Five asks from Traci — send permissions, LinkedIn outreach, research proposal, Alan Chong intro, Edmonton presentation.
speakerNotes: Five concrete asks. Most of them are quick — reviewing and sending emails you've already reviewed once. The bigger commitment is co-authoring the research study, which I'd love to discuss today.
